75746.subject: ⚠️ OpenAI stole this from Claude
75746.body:
----------
######                  [Collaborate with us](https://utm.genai.works/r/he2t9cz)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** OpenAI showed up Thursday to launch its new models and dropped a whole AI agent too, one that looks an awful lot like Claude. Meta put out a coding model at a quarter of everyone else's price. Ben Bernanke, who ran the Fed through the 2008 crash, joined the body that oversees Anthropic. And Google built a locked room where AI can run its own code without touching yours.



**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **OpenAI:** What did OpenAI drop alongside GPT-5.6?

* **Meta:** How cheap is Meta's new coding model?

* **Anthropic:** Why did a former Fed chair just join Anthropic?

* **Google:** Where is all that AI-written code supposed to run?


----------



----------
## [OpenAI turned ChatGPT into Claude Cowork](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/869e4f1b-d72b-432d-ad09-28a0a1ede84b/Beehiiv_01_-_Jul_10.png?t=1783676054)
Caption: 

Thursday was supposed to be about the GPT-5.6 models going public. OpenAI used the moment to drop something bigger. 

ChatGPT Work is an agent you hand a goal, and it works across your apps and files for hours before handing back a finished deck, sheet or dashboard.

On desktop it reads your local files, drives its own built-in browser, and clicks and types across your apps on its own. 

It plugs into Slack, Gmail, Salesforce, GitHub and a dozen more, so you can point it at real work. One company turned it loose on their sales pipeline and it dug up seven figures in missed deals.

To cram all this in, OpenAI folded Codex into a new desktop app and renamed the old one ChatGPT Classic. 

It's Claude Cowork with more muscle, and Anthropic saw it coming, rushing Cowork onto phones two days earlier and resetting all user limits yesterday. OpenAI's version digs deeper into your computer. It just wrecked the chat box getting there and stopped supporting their Atlas browser.


----------











----------
_Special highlight from our network_

## [Most people use 5% of Claude. Master the rest in one weekend.](https://link.genai.works/rx1t)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/23443f55-16da-403b-82dd-faf67791382f/Outskill_July_8.JPG?t=1783501969)
Caption: 

Most professionals open Claude, ask a question, and close the tab. That's about 5% of what Claude can actually do in 2026.

The other 95% is where the interesting work is happening. Skills t

75745.subject: The Ment Tech Intelligence Brief
75745.body:
Ment Tech | Meant To Innovate

Автор: Ment Tech Labs


Автор: Ment Tech Labs

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ment-tech-intelligence-brief-menttechlabs-bsocf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B08UiPGk3Tl2iIZV60B2Opw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0qZsaamTX8Dck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrer0ufd~p2-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrer0ufd-p2
  
There are five developments this week. Each one changes the operating environment for every company building on AI and blockchain infrastructure. Here is what every founder, enterprise operator and investor needs to understand. ◆ DEVELOPMENT 01 : 30 To 46 Percent Of Enterprise AI Token Volume In The US Is Now Flowing To Chinese Models. This Is Not A Geopolitical Story. It Is A Pricing Story. CNBC published a major investigation on July 7 confirming that 30 to 46 percent of enterprise AI token usage at US companies is flowing to Chinese open-weight models. The driver is not ideology. It is economics. For routine tasks summarization, code completion, data extraction, customer support drafting models that cost 60 to 90 percent less and perform within 5 to 10 percentage points of frontier Western models are the rational choice. The advisor model technique is becoming mainstream: a cheap open-weight model handles the bulk of tasks, and a frontier model is only invoked when the performance gap is decisive. Why it changes the build environment: The enterprise AI market is bifurcating. Western frontier models are pricing themselves out of the middle tier of enterprise workflows and ceding that market to Chinese open-weight alternatives. The enterprises that modeled their AI cost structure on frontier pricing for every task are now facing a structural mismatch between what they committed to and what the market will support. The Ment Tech perspective: We design client AI infrastructure around model-agnostic routing from day one precisely because this bifurcation was visible before it became a headline. The clients getting this right are the ones whose architecture can route between frontier and open-weight models without a rebuild. The ones getting it wrong are the ones locked into a single provider's pricing. ◆ DEVELOPMENT 02 OpenAI Offered Washington A 5% Equity Stake In The Company. The Government Moved From Gatekeeper To Potential Shareholder In Frontier AI In A Single Week. OpenAI floated giving Washington a 5% equity stake while its unreleased flagship GPT-5.6 stayed under federal review. An ownership stake would formally align the incentives of the leading commercial lab and its regulator, with downstream effects on model access, export policy, and competitive dynamics. Coming alongside the Fable 5 restoration and a delayed frontier rollo

CMC Market Pulse:
The Essential Weekly Crypto Briefing

Welcome to this week’s CMC Market Pulse newsletter!

CMC Market Pulse delivers actionable market insights, giving you
all the key narratives and developments you need in under 5
minutes.

Let's dive into this week.

Market Overview 📈
-----------------

Bitcoin (BTC) continues to squeeze higher, pushing out a
positive
3.7%
return this week. Ethereum (ETH) traded in line with Bitcoin,
rising
3.38%
in the same period. Total crypto market cap followed suit as
well, rising to
$2.19T,
up
1.28%
from $2.14T in the week before.

Weekly liquidations continued to remain low across the week,
with liquidations leaning toward short positions, peaking at
$282M liquidated on Monday. Across the board, funding rates
remain largely stable, with a slight increase across majors.
Despite the odd altcoin with overly high or low funding rates,
the majority remain within expectations.

* Michael Saylor’s Strategy sells 3,588 BTC for $216M, bringing
their total holdings to 843,775 BTC.
* Coinbase obtains the MiFID license in the United Kingdom, which
will enable UK users to trade derivatives and equities alongside
crypto.
* Sony has received approval to establish a U.S. national trust
bank subsidiary to support the issuance of dollar-denominated
stablecoins.
Traditional markets show some slow signs of recovery from
the prior week’s selloff on AI-related trades, whi
